All right, welcome back everybody. This will be a bit more of an advanced strategy that might feel like there's a bit of a learning curve to implement, but once you do, it's actually quite easy and simple and can really give you a proactive way of engaging with your highest intent leads in that critical final 48 hour window. This is called the Minority Report Reachout and I'm going to give you a bit more detail as soon as I share my screen. So let's do it. So Minority Report Reachout. What's with the weird name? Well, there was this movie with Tom Cruise based off of, I think it was like a Philip K. Dick novel called Minority Report. And I don't remember all the details, but I remember the core premise was essentially like Tom Cruise was on this police force that was on preemptive crimes, right? And they knew somebody was about to commit a murder or a crime before they actually did it and they would arrest them preemptively. So I don't know if that just kind of stuck out and I don't know. I like the idea of calling this the Minority Report Reachout. But the whole essence of it is that there are conversion signals or tells that we could identify or that our automations can identify that give us an indication of someone's level of intent and potentiality for converting on our offer. And these are the ones that we want our automations to be able to signal so that we can intervene with that conversion coaching and do what we can to encourage that decisiveness and level of support to push them across the line, not push them, but just lead them across that line. So that's the idea of this, right? Obviously on a large list, 50,000, 60,000, 100,000, we're not going to be able to reach out to even 1% of people on that list. And that's why we use things like automation and in this case, lead scoring in particular to know who within that list would most benefit from that individual reach out. So that is the idea between Minority Report Marketing and at a high level what you're going to be doing in a launch context specifically that you're going to list, sort your list or your CRM. We use Active Campaign by the highest lead scores and those who have not yet, those who have not yet bought, so you're going to want to filter out those who have the purchase tag for this program so that you're not sending individual reach outs, encouraging decisiveness and those who've already made a decision because that would be weird. But you're going to sort your list by the highest lead scores and send personal reach outs that could be via video like a loom or a dub or a bonjoro. It could be a personal email. It could be a DM on Messenger or Facebook if that's tool you use. And you're going to do that in the final 48 hours to anyone who has either passed a certain predetermined threshold like anyone over a lead score of 30 or you could simply send it to your top 20 or 50 scores or whatever you and your team have allocated bandwidth for doing. So why are we doing this? Well, these are going to be your highest intent leads who by the actions they've taken or the signals they've shown have demonstrated a closeness to the conversion where a short personal intervention or conversion coaching may be that thing that gives them the confidence to move forward. One of the reasons this works is the concept of anti anonymity, right? Sometimes simply being seen and accounted for within the launch like the creator, the author knows you and is signaling like, hey, what's up, Bill? I just wanted to see where you're at. Sometimes simply being accounted for is enough to get someone to make a decision where they feel accountable to a decision versus anonymous within this big launch ecosystem. And then essentially what we're doing is we're trying to reverse engineer your yeses to create your lead, your launch lead scoring rules. So one exercise you can go through with your team is see those who have joined your program and play the what was true about them exercise. What was true about their journey and their path in terms of like the emails they consumed or the way they've self segmented within your funnel. What's been true and how can you reverse engineer that into tells that get tracked throughout your launch or funnel? So that is more in depth. That's a bit more advanced. That's something we cover in our program automated intimacy for the purpose of just having a very solid foundation within your launch sequence. A basic starting point would be tracking email opens and lead scoring that lead scoring link clicks that I put clinks. I'm thinking about like scotch really early in the morning. No, link clicks. Sorry. Tracking key page visit. So anyone who has clicked to your sales page and then gone to the cart. If someone's in your cart, that's about as high and as it gets. That is certainly something to track webinar registrations as well. Email replies as well. So we have suggested basic scoring rules for those email clicks and opens at five each. A sales page visit at 12, a car visit at 20, a webinar registration at 10 and email replied seven. And you could either, as I said, if you have a really massive list, make it that so that those who cross a certain threshold, get that one on one message or that direct reach out. Or if you're operating with a smaller list, literally just rank out, you know, the top 1520 and work from top to bottom. So that is essentially what we are doing as a whole. So if you have any comments on how to set this up and just best ways to navigate through lead scoring. Yeah, so a few comments. Lead scoring, conversion context, all this is a vast, vast conversation. And as Ryan said, we go way into this in AI. But I think our intent here is that if you were just to kind of do what Ryan just shared with you, like start looking at these kind of five kind of fundamental aspects that you could be tracking that are pretty common across all businesses, you could probably have some pretty, pretty quick wins. And so I will be adding a supplemental video. We won't be going into the depths of how to set this all up because it really is a much broader conversation. And we want what you really want to do is you want to over time really fine tune this to your audience and your business. But I do at least want you to be familiar in your systems of like, hey, how do I get at this information? Because even knowing how to get at that information is going to be a great starting point for you. And then, yeah, like once you have these things in place, of course, you could start running all sorts of advanced automations. And there will be a time and place for that. But the most simple, the simplest use case is quite literally just to go like during a launch, you're down to those last 48 hours. You go into your list and you say, hey, show me all of my contacts who haven't muted out. You know, we've talked about this concept of muting out in this in MBL. So let me see all those people. Let me see all the people that haven't muted out, haven't made the purchase. And then you sort by lead score. So if you put these these factors into place and, you know, chances are if you just start at the top of that list and you say, OK, let me start with the person that has the highest score out of my entire list in that category, you're talking to the right person. And if you just work your way down that list, there's almost guaranteed to be gold there. And I know from personal experiences, running launches for others, as well as launches in Empire Engineering, when you go back after a launch and you correlate those scores to your buyers, it's you're almost always looking at the same list. So it's a really beautiful practice to put into place. Yep. One last note, all that is right. Like a lot of people that I've suggested to feel a little bit kind of like awkward sending these messages, right? Almost like they're afraid of being rejected or making someone uncomfortable or afraid of being pushy. And I get all that. I am super awkward and directly just. But I do want to make it clear, right, that those you are sending this to, they are actively engaged in your launch. They are actively engaged in a decision making process. The reason you are being they are being signaled to you is because they are currently consuming your emails. They're reading your sales page. They're on your checkout card. Like they are right there. So it's almost like for their experience, when they get a personalized video message from the creator, who they've just experienced in 2D, right, on the sales page, on these things, they've just read your email, it feels a synchronistic like, holy shit, I was just like reading your email and now I'm getting a personal video from you. Like that's pretty wild. Yeah. And they're already relating to you in a positive way by investing their time energy in you and your content and your emails and your sales page. So to get something from you, you have to remember will be welcomed. It's going to be exceptionally timely because they're actively engaged in that decision making process. And once again, sometimes just knowing that you're relating to them in a one-on-one way and you see them and they're accounted for and non-anonymous. That will often be enough so you don't have to make a hard sell out of it. It's really just a simple 30 second video of like, hey, Phil, I just really appreciate you tuning in to this launch and giving our program a consideration. I want you to know that my team and I are here and available. Anything we could do to help you make a confident decision in the next day or so. Let us know. Right. Like that's it. 20 to 30 seconds centered around you being in service of them making a confident decision. And yeah, put it to use. Yeah. I mean, a physical analogy here would be like if someone walked into your brick and mortar store, if they were standing there kind of by the checkout area, just looking around at things, of course you would greet them, right? Like it would just be your human tendency, right? We're going to say hello and acknowledge the people that are there in front of us. So that's the way I see this. You know, what we're not doing is we're not in no offense, no judgment to the guys that are doing this, but we're not like the guy standing out on the street corner spinning the tax sign or like dropping the flyers on your windshield or knocking on your door cold. Like it's none of that. Like the jet's a completely different context, right? Like these are people that are expecting and in desire to hear from you. So exactly. Cool. So yeah, that's really the strategy as a whole. I'd recommend, even if it feels a little bit of a stretch and advance right now, to implement it even in a basic way on the lead scoring rules, because being able to identify those top 20 that you can reach out to in those final 48 hours, you know, especially if it's a mid to high ticket package that could really move the needle on something that doesn't take that much time to set up and implement and work through. So yeah, once again, this kind of carries over from our concept of using your launch as scaffolding, right? It provides information that allows you to create more individualized, personalized touch points that we call conversion coaching. So yes, your launch will convert a good amount. But even if all your launch was doing was providing you those signals, those tells of who's opening, who's on a checkout page, who's crossing these lead score thresholds, and now you get to have one-on-one conversations with those who just need to be taken from like that seven on 10 to that eight on 10 or to that 10 on 10. That's really time well spent and high ROI time in your business. So yeah, that's all we got on this one. Let's take a pause and we'll meet you in the final lesson. Bye, everyone.
